'''Lott Kemp''' was a [[Gender/Legends|male]] [[Crystal/Legends|crystal]] [[Merchant/Legends|merchant]]<ref name="The Destroyer" /> from the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Dodz]].<ref name="CSWE" /> Through the [[Intergalactic Droid Agency]], Kemp acquired [[C-3PO/Legends|C-3PO]] and [[R2-D2/Legends|R2-D2]] as [[Service droid/Legends|servant droids]]. However, before they arrived, Kemp came under pressure to pay heavy taxes to the corrupt [[Governor/Legends|Governor]] [[Kugg]]. He refused to pay the wanted amount, so Kugg sent his [[Battle droid/Legends|killer droid]], the [[Destroyer (droid)|Destroyer]], to "deal with" Kemp at the crystal merchant's [[Dwelling of Lott Kemp|residence]]. Although he escaped with his life<ref name="The Destroyer" /> and fled off-world to avoid the deadly droid,<ref name="The Droids Re-Animated, Part 1">{{SW|url=news/the-droids-re-animated-part-1|series=The Droids Re-Animated|text=Part 1|nolive=1}}</ref> the Destroyer decimated Kemp's dwelling.<ref name="The Destroyer" />

When R2 and 3PO arrived at Kemp's residence, they found it destroyed and feared their master to be [[Death/Legends|dead]]. However, [[Jost Ellon]] was looting the rubble and, after informing them of Kemp's survival, became the droids' new master.<ref name="The Destroyer" />

==Behind the scenes==
Lott Kemp was first mentioned in [[Droids (1986) 1|''Droids'' (1986) 1]], the first issue of the [[Star Wars: Droids (1986)|''Star Wars Droids'']] comic series published by [[Star Comics]], an imprint of [[Marvel Comics]],<ref name="The Destroyer" /> which was first published on [[April 1]], [[1986]].<ref name="The Destroyer publication date">{{Marvel|url=comics/issue/54151/star_wars_droids_1986_1|text=Star Wars: Droids (1986) #1}}</ref>
